channel.title: Tutorial: Posts are getting error Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function
channel.description:
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->
Finished the Tutorial but getting error on the Posts:
> Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: undefined. You likely forgot to export your component from the file it's defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.
Did I miss an export somewhere? https://github.com/muckitymuck/autismkidslife.org/tree/development/autismkidslife.org

item.title: Tutorial: Posts are getting error Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function
item.dc:creator: Hussein
item.description:
hi! I tried your code and seems to be working. Did you manage to solve the issue? This normally happens with you try to import a class from a javascript file but the file has a different name or doesn’t have an export. Let us know if you still have the issue

Read full topic
item.source: Tutorial: Posts are getting error Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function

item.title: Tutorial: Posts are getting error Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function
item.dc:creator: andrewgoldstein
item.description:
Finished the Tutorial but getting error on the Posts:

Element type is invalid: expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: undefined. You likely forgot to export your component from the file it’s defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.

Did I miss an export somewhere?

github.com

autismkidslife.org/autismkidslife.org at development ·...

development/autismkidslife.org

Contribute to muckitymuck/autismkidslife.org development by creating an account on GitHub.

Read full topic
